summary.intent: Fix probe/validation error messages in model selectors to show inline errors with accurate, model-specific text instead of generic messages.
summary.outcome: Inline error display was added to the Preferred Models selector in providers.js (line 1248); error message fidelity was improved so 'permission' errors preserve the original message while HTTP 403/forbidden errors still show generic text.

repo: There are at least two separate model selector UI components: one in the onboarding flow and one in the Preferred Models section of providers.js — both needed the same inline error treatment.
Error badge rendering in the Preferred Models card uses a 'provider-item-badge warning' span with the error only in the title attribute (tooltip), not inline visible text.

code.finding: Model card rendering puts probe errors in the title attribute only; inline error div was added here to make errors visible without hover.

workflow: Fixing the same UI pattern in one location (settings page) does not automatically fix sibling components — each distinct rendering path must be patched separately.
The user verified the fix by inspecting raw HTML of the rendered component, which revealed the error was in title only.

friction: First push was believed to be complete but the user still saw the bug — the fix had only been applied to onboarding, not to the Preferred Models selector in providers.js, requiring a second investigation and fix.
open_items: Consider unifying the model card rendering logic across onboarding and Preferred Models selectors to avoid needing duplicate fixes in the future.
